creator: cyberbotx@cyberbotx.com
text:
Actually, this error was corrected already, I submitted a patch to it that was 
just committed earlier today.  Update your ports tree and try it again.

Naram Qashat

Rudy Rucker wrote:
>> Number:         119847
>> Category:       ports
>> Synopsis:       mencoder / mplayer doesn't build  (vorbis error)
>> Confidential:   no
>> Severity:       non-critical
>> Priority:       high
>> Responsible:    freebsd-ports-bugs
>> State:          open
>> Quarter:        
>> Keywords:       
>> Date-Required:
>> Class:          sw-bug
>> Submitter-Id:   current-users
>> Arrival-Date:   Mon Jan 21 01:10:01 UTC 2008
>> Closed-Date:
>> Last-Modified:
>> Originator:     Rudy Rucker
>> Release:        FreeBSD 7.0-PRERELEASE i386
>> Organization:
>> Environment:
> 
> 
> System: FreeBSD 7.0-PRERELEASE #2: Thu Jan  3 15:59:14 PST 2008
>     root@monchichi.monkeybrains.net:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/MONCHICHI
> 
> 
> 
>> Description:
> 
> 
> 
> mplayer/mencoder don't build.
> 
> The problem has been discussed on the FreeBSD-ports mailing list.
> http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2007-December/045625.html
> 
> ad_libvorbis.c: In function 'decode_audio':
> ad_libvorbis.c:232: warning: passing argument 2 of 'ds_get_packet_pts' from incompatible pointer type
> ad_libvorbis.c:238: error: too few arguments to function 'vorbis_synthesis'
> gmake[1]: *** [ad_libvorbis.o] Error 1
> gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer/work/MPlayer-1.0rc2/libmpcodecs'
> gmake: *** [libmpcodecs/libmpcodecs.a] Error 2
> *** Error code 2
> 
> Stop in /usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer.
> *** Error code 1
> 
> Stop in /usr/ports/multimedia/mplayer.
> 
> 
> 
>> How-To-Repeat:
> 
> 
> 
> Update your ports tree, try to build mencoder or mplayer.
> 
> 
>> Fix:
> 
> 
> 
> This fix is here, and has not been added to the Makefile for mencoder/mplayer yet.  I don't understand why this works...
> http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-ports/2007-December/045885.html
> 
>
